title: 'Distorted picture of India': MEA slams US report recommending sanctions on RSS, R&AW
content: India has strongly rejected the US Commission on International Religious Freedom's 2026 report, calling it biased and distorted. The MEA spokesperson criticized the commission's reliance on questionable sources and ideological narratives, urging it to address religious freedom issues within the US. India maintains its commitment to religious harmony.

title: Dhoni next India coach? Gambhir's role reversal wish after Instagram exchange
content: Speaking about the exchange a week after the final, Gambhir said he appreciated Dhoni's message and hoped to return the gesture one day.

title: Google, Amazon, OpenAI and 5 others sign pledge to share AI safety practices
content: Major tech companies including Google, Amazon, and OpenAI have signed the "Online Services Accord Against Scams" to share threat intelligence and combat online fraud. This voluntary pledge aims to foster industry-wide collaboration with governments and law enforcement to detect and prevent scams more effectively. The initiative seeks to accelerate communication and coordinated actions against evolving threat actors.

title: Medico suicide case: 5 AIIMS Rajkot students arrested; 17-page note alleges assault by batchmates
content: Five final-year MBBS students from AIIMS Rajkot were arrested for abetting the suicide of a classmate, Ratan Kumar Meghwal. Meghwal died by train on March 14, leaving a 17-page note detailing alleged physical assault and mental harassment by the accused since January.

title: 'I’ve spoken to Mahi bhai': Samson breaks silence on 1st time with Dhoni
content: Sanju Samson said he is excited to play alongside MS Dhoni for the first time in the Indian Premier League, calling it a big learning opportunity. The wicketkeeper-batter also reflected on India’s triumph in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026, where he was Player of the Tournament. Meanwhile, Mohammed Siraj called his unexpected inclusion in the title-winning squad “a miracle.”

title: FASTag annual pass gets pricier: Here's what changes from April 1
content: The National Highways Authority of India is increasing the FASTag Annual Pass fee. Starting April 1, 2026, the pass will cost Rs 3,075. This prepaid toll plan is for non-commercial private vehicles. The revision aligns with National Highways Fee Rules. Over 56 lakh users have adopted the pass. The new rate applies to eligible vehicles at approximately 1,150 fee plazas.

title: UK will not be drawn into ‘wider war’ in Middle East, says PM Keir Starmer
content: Britain will not join a wider Middle East war, Prime Minister Keir Starmer stated, despite collaborating with allies on a plan to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  He emphasized restoring shipping flows and global market stability, ruling out a NATO mission.  Starmer stressed the need for a collective, viable plan, not a direct military conflict.

title: Divyanka Tripathi and Vivek Dahiya expecting their first child - Exclusive
content: Divyanka Tripathi and Vivek Dahiya are set to welcome their first child, bringing immense joy to their families. The couple, who met on the sets of Yeh Hai Mohabbatein, are also expected to celebrate the occasion with a baby shower soon.

title: Suvendu vs Mamata? BJP releases candidate list for 144 Bengal seats
content: The BJP has unveiled its initial candidate list for 144 West Bengal assembly seats, featuring prominent figures like Suvendu Adhikari, who will contest from Bhabanipur and Nandigram, challenging Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee. Dilip Ghosh is set to run from Kharagpur Sadar. These announcements precede the two-phase elections scheduled for April 23 and 29, with vote counting on May 4.

title: Nvidia CEO seemingly 'realises' that Google, Microsoft and Meta are set to eat lunch
content: Nvidia's dominance built on a single chip for all AI tasks is facing a challenge.  As inference, the running of AI models, becomes more crucial and cost-sensitive, customers are exploring cheaper, purpose-built alternatives from rivals like Google and Meta.  Jensen Huang's expected unveiling of a new inference-focused chip signals a potential shift away from Nvidia's long-held strategy.

title: 'When they are shot, I will be at peace': Delhi Uttam Nagar murder victim's mother demands justice
content: A 26-year-old man was killed in Delhi's Uttam Nagar after a Holi celebration dispute over a water balloon escalated into violence. His mother demands strict action, alleging the accused threatened her granddaughter and assaulted her family. The family seeks severe punishment for the perpetrators.

title: Rasgulla stuck in throat: Man chokes to death at wedding feast
content: A tragic incident unfolded at a wedding feast in Jamshedpur, Jharkhand, where a 41-year-old man, Lalit Singh, succumbed to death. A rasgulla reportedly became lodged in his throat, completely blocking his airway and cutting off oxygen supply. He collapsed during the meal and tragically passed away within minutes despite immediate efforts to seek medical assistance.

title: Kyle Daukaus says he doesn't deserve UFC White House card, talks Bo Nickal fight
content: ​Unranked middleweight fighter Kyle Daukaus has openly admitted that he understands the criticism surrounding his place in the historic UFC event scheduled at the White House. The fighter is set to face rising prospect Bo Nickal on the undercard of a blockbuster show planned to celebrate the 80th birthday of Donald Trump. The event is expected to take place on the South Lawn and mark the 250th anniversary celebration of the United States.

title: $3.4 trillion mistake with China: Why US will make India's rise far tougher
content: The US has spent a staggering $3.4 trillion on a "militarised rivalry" with China since 2012, a figure dwarfing its Afghanistan war costs. This massive peacetime spending, driven by a strategic pivot to Asia, highlights Washington's determination to avoid repeating past "mistakes" with Beijing, particularly concerning technological transfers and economic engagement, as it now recalibrates its approach with India.

title: 'Jana Nayagan' censor row: Revising committee to watch Thalapathy Vijay’s film on THIS date
content: Thalapathy Vijay's 'Jana Nayagan' faces a revised censor screening on March 17th after earlier postponements. This crucial step follows court intervention and ongoing certification delays, which have also impacted the film's OTT deal. High expectations surround the movie, potentially Vijay's last before his political focus, as fans await a release date post-certification.

title: How Azhar Mahmood played IPL despite Pakistan player ban since 2009
content: Mahmood played for Punjab Kings (then Kings XI Punjab) in the 2012 and 2013 seasons and later represented KKR in the 2015 season.

title: Tuberculosis can silently damage male fertility: Experts explain risks
content: Tuberculosis can silently affect male fertility by damaging reproductive organs, leading to reduced sperm health and potential infertility. Early recognition of subtle symptoms like testicular pain or blood in semen is crucial for timely diagnosis and treatment, which can often restore reproductive function.

title: 'In the name of surgery': Afridi blasts Pak selectors for dropping Babar & Co.
content: Shahid Afridi criticised Pakistan’s selectors after the Pakistan national cricket team lost the ODI series 2–1 to the Bangladesh national cricket team following a poor ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 campaign. Afridi blamed excessive experimentation and sidelining experienced players. Bangladesh secured the series with an 11-run win at Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium despite Salman Ali Agha’s century for Pakistan.

title: A property fight that lasted 76 yrs: Here's how Bombay HC settled the matter
content: A 76-year-old family property dispute, initiated shortly after India's Constitution was enacted, has finally concluded. The Bombay High Court ordered a five-way partition of a one-acre plot in Yerawada, Pune, resolving the long-standing battle between heirs of landowner Mia Mohamed Haji Janmohamed Chotani.

title: 'Have almost 100% attendance': Tharoor says Kerala polls may break his record - watch
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor anticipates missing the final two weeks of Parliament to campaign for the Kerala Assembly elections, a departure from his strong attendance record. He highlighted the crucial nature of the polls, aiming to unseat the ruling LDF. Tharoor also noted the surprise early election schedule, leaving parties with limited preparation time.

title: Maharashtra horror: Ex-lover abducts, rapes minor girl, ties her to pole at village chowk
content: A 17-year-old girl was allegedly kidnapped, raped, and tied to a pole overnight in Beed district. Police arrested her 29-year-old former boyfriend based on her statement. The incident highlights a disturbing act of violence and the subsequent legal proceedings initiated by the authorities.

title: Kerala polls: BJP's 1st list of 47 candidates out; fields Rajeev Chandrasekhar, K Surendran
content: The Bharatiya Janata Party has announced its initial list of 47 candidates for the upcoming Kerala assembly elections. Key leaders like Rajeev Chandrasekhar and K Surendran are among those fielded. The party aims to contest across various constituencies in the state. Kerala will go to polls on April 9, with results declared on May 4.

title: Watch: Fire erupts again at Fujairah port in UAE, harbour suspends oil loadings
content: Fujairah port in the UAE experienced a drone attack, igniting a fire at a petroleum facility. While emergency teams quickly contained the blaze with no reported injuries, oil loadings were suspended. This marks a recent escalation in attacks on the vital oil hub, underscoring regional tensions and the port's critical role in global energy trade.

title: ‘Fees paid to Pak players aid those killing Indians’: Gavaskar slams Abrar signing
content: Sunrisers Leeds' signing of Pakistani spinner Abrar Ahmed has ignited controversy in India, with Sunil Gavaskar urging Indian-owned franchises to avoid such deals. He argued that payments to Pakistani players indirectly fund their government, potentially harming India.  While the franchise cited cricketing reasons, the BCCI stated it cannot intervene in foreign league decisions.

title: NFL trade rumors: Jordan Love’s Green Bay Packers linked to $321m ex-Minnesota Vikings QB
content: ESPN’s Bill Barnwell suggests Green Bay Packers as the perfect landing spot for veteran quarterback Kirk Cousins. With Jordan Love’s seldom absences, Cousins could serve as a trusted backup or bridge starter. His extensive experience and constant motivation to play at a high level make him a valuable gem for Green Bay. If he wants a Super Bowl before retirement, then the Packers’ the possible place.

title: The 'files' of Bengaluru techies
content: Bengaluru's tech boom hides a dark reality of distress. Cases of software engineers dying by suicide or facing accusations of violence highlight isolation, marital conflict, and immense pressure.  From dowry harassment to startup failures, these tragedies expose the human cost behind urban professional success, demanding urgent attention to mental well-being and support systems.

title: ‘It was very harsh’: Surya on bold call to pick Ishan Kishan over Jitesh Sharma
content: Suryakumar Yadav revealed the T20 World Cup selection of Ishan Kishan over Jitesh Sharma was a gut call, believing Kishan was the "X-factor." Kishan justified this faith with strong performances, helping India win the tournament. The captain also highlighted Sanju Samson's impactful inclusion at the top order, which shifted the team's momentum.

title: Humpy likely to skip Women's Candidates 2026 - here's why
content: Koneru Humpy is considering withdrawing from the FIDE Candidates Tournament in Cyprus due to safety concerns linked to the ongoing conflict involving the United States, Israel, and Iran. While FIDE says the situation is calm and preparations continue, Humpy believes holding the crucial event near a conflict zone is risky and suggests considering alternative venues or dates.

title: Roger Federer image questioned as insider says Rafael Nadal felt more authentic
content: A Roland Garros insider, Gilles Jourdan, recently claimed Rafael Nadal appeared more authentic and approachable than Roger Federer during their careers. Speaking to L’Équipe, Jourdan said Federer seemed focused on business and money, while Nadal built stronger relationships at the tournament. Despite the criticism, Federer remains one of tennis’ greatest champions and a billionaire through endorsements and investments.

title: Planning to buy property in India as NRI? Here's why it might not be a good idea
content: Non-resident Indians investing heavily in Indian real estate face challenges as properties may not yield expected financial returns or security. Experts warn of obsolescence in older flats, complications with family-gifted properties, and reduced value due to currency depreciation. Managing these illiquid assets from abroad also proves difficult.

title: 'Targeted for being Hardeep Puri's daughter': Defamation plea in HC over Epstein posts
content: Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri's daughter has filed a plea in the Delhi High Court, seeking legal action against individuals and online platforms. She alleges defamatory content linking her to Jeffrey Epstein is being circulated, causing significant damage to her reputation. The petition requests an injunction against further publication, removal of existing material, and compensation for damages.

title: High-speed Royal Enfield crash in Lucknow: Teen rider collides with scooter, dies
content: A Class XII student died after his motorcycle crashed into a divider during a high-speed bike stunt near Janeshwar Mishra Park. The incident occurred when his bike collided with a scooter that suddenly appeared, causing him to lose control. The accident was captured on camera, and police are investigating.

title: 'I was declared dead': Woman shares her journey from death to recovery and how faith helped her
content: Komal Jamwar, diagnosed with Guillain-Barré syndrome, experienced rapid paralysis and was even declared dead. Her husband's persistent CPR and her family's unwavering support, coupled with her own strong will to live, were instrumental in her recovery. After months of rehabilitation, Komal regained her ability to walk, proving the power of hope and perseverance.

title: 'Panchayat' star Faisal Malik bags the role of Kumbhakarna in Ranbir Kapoor starrer
content: Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana buzzes: Faisal Malik (Panchayat's Prahlad) as Kumbhakarna, shot VFX-heavy scenes with Yash. Ranbir as Ram, Sai Pallavi Sita, Yash Ravana, Sunny Deol Hanuman. Part 1 Diwali 2026, part 2 2027.

title: No gas, no problem: Ready-made meals and frozen foods take over Indian homes
content: Middle East tensions are disrupting LPG supplies, pushing Indian households towards ready-to-cook meals and frozen snacks. Retailers report a significant surge in demand for these alternatives, with some anticipating a lasting shift in consumer habits. Despite government efforts, shortages persist, driving interest in electric cooking solutions.

title: Andhra Pradesh set to become first State to get Pramaan certification for forest produce
content: Certification under Indian Forest and Wood Certification Scheme likely for eucalyptus grown outside forests, expected to improve market access and returns for growers

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: LDF seeks third term, UDF readies to end 10-year drought; BJP a bystander
content: A good outing in the 2025 local body polls has energised the UDF that seeks to deny the LDF a hattrick in Kerala; amid welfare promises, the West Asia war may dominate political discourse; the BJP hopes to make a mark, having won a Lok Sabha seat and the Thiruvananthapuram corporation

title: JD(U) announces schedule for party president’s election; Nitish Kumar likely to be re-elected
content: According to party sources, nomination papers for the election will be filed on March 22 and can be withdrawn by March 24

title: Economic stagnation, job mismatch behind ‘distress migration’ from Punjab: Experts
content: Punjab’s Economic Survey 2025-26 notes that a combination of “pull” and “push” factors behind brain drain from the State.

title: Vellappally Natesan, son file appeal challenging HC order disqualifying them from holding posts in SNDP Yogam
content: The High Court disqualified them from their posts following a finding that they acted in violation of the Companies Act, 2013, by failing to file audited accounts of the Yogam for three consecutive years

title: BJP releases first list of 47 candidates for Assembly polls
content: Key candidates include State president Rajeev Chandrasekhar in Nemom, Union Minister George Kurian from from Kanjirappally and former Union Minister V. Muraleedharan in Kazhakuttom. With plans to boost its vote share following recent local successes, the BJP is gearing up for a significant electoral challenge in the State.

title: Tonga Experience Centre near Town Hall sparks heritage row in Mysuru
content: This is the second time in recent months that a structure proposed near Balarama Gate of the palace has run into trouble

title: S. Tamilselvan wins Sahitya Akademi award for literary criticism
content: Mr. Tamilselvan said there had been an attempt to destroy the autonomy of the Sahitya Akademi and that the announcement of the prize had been delayed by three months

title: Left Front announces first list of 192 candidates for West Bengal Assembly polls
content: Party veterans along with new emerging youth leaders feature in the list; the list includes 27 women candidates.

title: State govt. committed to economic and political empowerment of Muslims: CM
content: Naidu highlights welfare schemes, pensions and education support at Ramzan Iftar, and says allocation for minority welfare increased to ₹6,090 crore in FY 2026-27 Budget

title: Madara Mahasabha stages protest march seeking internal reservation among SCs
content: During the march, they raised slogans calling for the implementation of the Justice Nagamohan Das commission’s recommendations regarding internal reservations

title: Fishermen in crisis following shortage of commercial gas
content: Fishermen who used to depend on commercial gas cylinders for cooking during long days of deep-sea fishing are now struggling to cope with the crisis in the absence of any intervention by the Fisheries department

title: Woman jumps into the Bhima over social boycott in Yadgir village
content: Two of five accused taken into custody for mentally harassing her over the issue of her son having a relationship with a woman-relative

title: Assam election: Almost 50% voters in Assam are women
content: Filing of nominations for the single phase election on April 9 began on March 16, the State’s Chief Electoral Officer says

title: BRS plans to corner govt. on pre-poll promises, six guarantees in Assembly: KTR
content: Opposition party legislators pay tributes to martyrs before heading to Assembly, raise slogans against Govt.

title: 11 family health centres in Kozhikode declared ‘antibiotic smart’
content: The family health centres at Thiruvambady, Chekkiad, Iringal, Azhiyoor, Meppayyur, Chooloor, Eramangalam, Kodiyathur, Iruvallur, Kulathoor, and Moodadi are ‘antibiotic smart’ now

title: Karnataka transgender coalition writes open letter to Prime Minister opposing Amendment Bill
content: The coalition members pointed out that passing the amendment will not only put the rights of the larger transgender community at risk, but will limit the right to self-identification for newer generations of transgender persons.

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026 | The temple that returns to Kerala’s political arena every election
content: The Sabarimala Ayyappa Temple is yet again at the centre of political conversation ahead of another election season in the State; while the LDF govt. has held a Global Ayyappa Sangamam and backtracked on its earlier stance on women’s entry, the UDF has raised the alleged gold theft at the temple as a major issue

title: Future-ready education vital for Viksit Bharat 2047, says CEC Director
content: Prof. Manhas, remarked that while Artificial Intelligence is a powerful tool, its effectiveness ultimately depends on human input

title: ‘Swadeshi’ matters in building strategic leverage in evolving world order: V. Anantha Nageswaran
content: Says for building strategic resilience and strategic indispensability, competitiveness in manufacturing and export sectors matters

title: Wayanad Sky Island bird survey records 156 species
content: Birdwatchers were deployed across 11 base camps to survey different habitats of the sky islands located above 1,200 metres, including forests, grasslands, and sholas

title: Women themselves are responsible for their own progress, says scholar
content: Latha Pujar was speaking at an International Women’s Day programme organised by the Swami Vivekananda Youth Movement in Dharwad

title: All-round growth of A.P. is true tribute to Potti Sriramulu, says Chandrababu Naidu
content: On the occasion of the freedom fighter's 125th birth anniversary, Chief Minister unveils 58-foot 'Statue of Sacrifice' installed at Sakhamuru Park as part of the Sri Potti Sriramulu Memorial Park project; postal cover released, Amarajeevi's descendants felicitated

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026 | Twenty20’s bonhomie with BJP faces electoral test
content: The outfit has sought nearly 20 seats spreading across Kerala. However, local leadership of the BJP in Ernakulam and Thrissur have refused to yield their strongholds

title: Chinnaswamy Stadium confirmed to host IPL 2026; Karnataka government grants formal clearance
content: The turning point for the venue came after former Indian pacer from Karnataka Venkatesh Prasad was elected president of the KSCA

title: AIDYO collecting signatures seeking recruitment to vacant posts
content: Activists say that there are nearly 13 lakh posts vacant in both Central and State government departments

title: A.P. districts rank high in fertilizer consumption
content: Nandyal tops India in DAP use in 2025 while SPSR Nellore leads urea sales in the State; experts caution against excessive use due to long-term soil health risks

title: Sahitya Akademi announces literary awards for 2025
content: Eight books of poetry, four novels, six books of short stories, two essays, one literary criticism, one autobiography, and two memoirs have won

title: LPG tanker Shivalik reaches Gujarat’s Mundra Port after crossing Strait of Hormuz
content: Special Secretary at the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, Rajesh Kumar Sinha, said that the Vessel Jag Laadki, carrying 81,000 metrics tonnes of Murban crude oil, is safely enroute to India; he said it would reach to Mundra Port on March 17.

title: Mandya prepares for fully digital 2027 census
content: Dr. Kumar says the census process will also involve live monitoring through the Census Management and Monitoring System portal

title: T.N. Assembly poll: Election Commission orders posting of new SPs in Erode, Karur, Nagapattinam, Virudhunagar
content: The officers transferred out [of the districts] shall not be in any election related posts till the completion of election, the ECI communication stated

title: Nainar Nagenthran, G.K. Vasan criticise State govt. over alleged ganja-related incident at Cuddalore hospital
content: A group of men, reportedly under the influence of ganja, entered a government hospital in Kattumannarkoil and threatened patients and doctors

title: Shutdown, protest rallies in Ladakh over Statehood, Sixth Schedule demands
content: Hundreds of locals marched from Singay Namgyal Chowk up to Leh Polo Ground in Leh town, while protesters held a rally in Kargil town that was attended by hundreds of locals; this came even as activist Sonam Wangchuk was released from jail; the KDA and the LAB had called for the shutdown on March 16 in Ladakh

title: Of 15,200 candidates from Dakshina Kannada trained under PM Kaushal Vikas Yojana, only 8.45% placed
content: Of 8,491 candidates trained under the scheme in Udupi district, 8.63% placed, according to Union Govt

title: Miffed over Karnataka government’s failure to provide answers to questions, Speaker abruptly adjourns Assembly
content: Speaker holds meeting with Chief Minister and others; Siddaramaiah asks Chief Secretary to issue strict instructions to secretaries of all departments to provide replies promptly to questions raised in Legislature

title: Watch: Model Code of Conduct in force in Tamil Nadu | ₹1.26 Cr Cash Seized on Day 1
content: Chief Electoral Officer Archana Patnaik said 2,106 flying squads and 23 enforcement agencies are monitoring violations

title: Early Chola-period sculptures, inscriptions found on river bed
content: Sculptures of Dakshinamurthy and Vinadhara Shiva found; researchers find stone slab with couple of inscriptions recording the gifts donated to a temple

title: India ‘engaged’ with U.S. on trade deal, says Commerce Secretary as officials warn of delays
content: Sources in the Ministry of Commerce and Industry say India will sign the deal only once the final “tariff architecture” of the U.S. becomes clear; this comes against the backdrop of a temporary 10% tariff by the U.S. and two new investigations it has launched on trade partners, including India.

title: TVK denies alliance talks with BJP, AIADMK, Sasikala and Ramadoss
content: Claims linking the party with BJP, AIADMK, Sasikala and Ramadoss over possible alliance discussions are “completely false”, says C.T.R. Nirmal Kumar

title: Maharashtra CM defends Bill against fraudulent religious conversions, cites exploitation of women
content: The Opposition parties are politicising the issue for vote-bank gains, but once they read the Bill carefully, they will have no objections, says Devendra Fadnavis

title: Boney Kapoor, Janhvi Kapoor, and Khushi Kapoor move Madras High Court over Sridevi’s Chennai property
content: The veteran actress had purchased the property by way of four sale deeds in 1988; however, it is now mired in controversy due to a claim for a share by another family

title: Political slugfest erupts in Delhi over Election Commission’s move to transfer Chief Secretary, DGP in poll-bound Bengal
content: Leaders of the ruling Trinamool Congress also criticised the poll panel’s move, with party MP Sougata Roy terming it “wrong” and “a step against West Bengal”

title: Palla flays YSRCP for obstructing development through legal cases
content: During the five-year tenure of the YSRCP government, the State witnessed exodus of industries and as many as 913 companies left A.P., TDP State president said reacting to YSRCP MP filing a PIL against the Google AI Hub and Data Center in Visakhapatnam

title: Foreign Medical Graduates’ association demands withdrawal of NMC notification
content: The association said that FMGs have expressed concern regarding the new “retrograde injustice” order issued by the NMC on March 6, which was presented as a clarification regarding mandatory physical compensation classes

title: Hard Rock International announces closure of 10 of its outlets in India, including three cafes in Bengaluru
content: The Hard Rock Hotel in Goa, which is connected to a different operator, would remain open, said Hard Rock International in a statement.

title: Team USA stifles D.R.'s offense to make WBC final
content: The United States held the Dominican Republic's vaunted offense in check to advance to the World Baseball Classic final with a 2-1 victory.

title: D.R. frustrated by blown call, but 'part of the game'
content: Two hours after a blown ball-strike call ended the Dominican Republic's World Baseball Classic, players emerged from the team's clubhouse with smiles, music and a message.

title: Sources: NBA eyes key 1st vote for expansion
content: The NBA will hold a vote at the board of governors meetings next week to explore adding expansion teams exclusively in Las Vegas and Seattle, sources told ESPN.

title: Duke top men's seed as Miami (OH) sneaks in
content: Duke, Arizona, Michigan and Florida were named the top seeds in the men's NCAA tournament with one-loss Miami (Ohio) grabbing an at-large bid.

title: UConn overall 1-seed; joined by UCLA, Texas, SC
content: Undefeated UConn was awarded the top overall seed in the women's NCAA tournament Sunday. The Huskies are joined by UCLA, Texas and South Carolina as the other No. 1 seeds.

title: Oddsmakers again pick Duke as king of NCAA field
content: Duke, Michigan and Arizona have separated themselves from the rest of the field as betting favorites to win the NCAA men's tournament in the eyes of oddsmakers.

title: WNBA, union end 6th day of talks with no deal
content: WNBA commissioner Cathy Engelbert said progress is being made toward a new collective bargaining agreement despite a sixth day of marathon talks with no deal.

title: 🏀 Men's NCAA tourney: Get to know every team
content: The men's bracket has been revealed. Here's what you need to know about every competitor for the national title.

content: A star-studded World Baseball Classic showdown lived up to the hype. Here's how the U.S. moved on to the final.

content: The Huskies are six wins away from a 13th title. Coaches and analysts gave us a blueprint for how to beat them.

title: March Madness 101: NCAA tournament format, history, facts
content: Who coined the term "Bracketology?" What is a "Cinderella" team? How do March Madness squares work? Check out fun facts on the NCAA tournament now.

content: Are J.J. McCarthy and Michael Penix Jr. officially QB2s? Will Maxx Crosby and A.J. Brown actually leave their current teams? We sized up five free agency takes.

content: We're following NFL free agency signings, trades, cuts, contract restructures and other moves as they happen.

content: Will any of the wild cards make a run? Plus, full playoff projections, draft lottery standings, more.

title: 'Glass in my hand': Inside Marcus Smart's incredible return from The Punch
content: After a devastating, self-inflicted wound nearly cost him his right hand, the Lakers guard is fueling the team's rise through the West.

title: Winners and losers from the 2026 Players Championship
content: From Cameron Young to Rory McIlroy, here's a look back at who performed well and who didn't in the Players Championship.

title: Who will face the U.S. in the WBC final? Keys, X factors for Italy-Venezuela
content: We've got you covered with predictions, live updates and takeaways from the second semifinal in Miami.

title: Arbeloa's youth revolution at Real Madrid is straight from Pep's playbook
content: Promoted from the B team, Álvaro Arbeloa has overseen the biggest influx of young talent into Real Madrid's first team in living memory. Sound familiar?

content: Moneylines, spreads, totals and BPI predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA men's tournament.

title: As it happened: Antonelli wins in Shanghai, Mercedes 1-2 again
content: Kimi Antonelli converted his record-breaking Chinese Grand Prix pole into a maiden Formula 1 victory on Sunday, you can relive exactly how it happened here.

title: Hamlin roars back to win Vegas after early penalty
content: Denny Hamlin overcame an early speeding penalty and drove through the field in a fairly dominating win for Joe Gibbs Racing and Toyota.

title: What is 3:16 Day? 'Stone Cold' Steve Austin's day explained
content: 3:16 Day celebrates the iconic legacy of WWE superstar "Stone Cold" Steve Austin. Learn more about its history.

title: 'Banana Brees': Former Saints QB Drew Brees and Tyler Shough take in Savannah Bananas
content: Brees and Shough made appearances during the Savannah Bananas exhibition games at the Caesars Superdome, the home of the Saints.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Real Madrid plot move for Bayern's Olise
content: Real Madrid are plotting a deal for Bayern star Michael Olise, while Chelsea want to sign Messi... just not the Inter Miami one. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Cheat sheet: Sleepers, busts, rookies and closers
content: You've read their names all preseason long, it'd be a shame to forget them on draft day. The ESPN Cheat Sheet is one way to make sure that doesn't happen.

title: How US Operation In Venezuela Differs From Iran: Fareed Zakaria Explains
content: The United States military intervention in Iran, now in its third week, stands in contrast to the recent operation in Venezuela, according to journalist and geopolitical expert Fareed Zakaria.

title: Netanyahu Posts New Video After Grok Flags His Cafe Clip As AI-Generated'
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has shared another video on the social media platform X (formerly Twitter) after Grok, the AI chatbot created by Elon Musk, labelled one of his earlier videos as "AI-generated".

title: Opinion: Opinion | The Story Of Bengal's 'Real' Outsiders
content: Bengal has always had a rich tradition of enabling 'outsiders', or non-Bangla speakers, including those making it to the Lok Sabha.

title: Not Just Bengal, Election Commission Transferred Officers In Tamil Nadu And Assam
content: The Election Commission of India has stepped up administrative changes in election-bound states, by removing several district police chiefs in both Assam and Tamil Nadu as part of its efforts to ensure free and fair polls.

title: "Anti-Women, Anti-Bengali": Mamata Banerjee Slams Poll Body Over Officers' Removal
content: Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee today attacked the BJP and the Election Commission over the removal of top bureaucrats in West Bengal.

title: Need To Get Out Of Mindset That Only Delhi Requires Green Cover: Supreme Court
content: There is a need to get out of the mindset that only the national capital needs to have greenery and other states are lesser mortals, the Supreme Court said on Monday while hearing a matter related to the Delhi Ridge.

title: NDTV Exclusive: Rifle, Tactical Gear And A Long Wait - Inside The Kill Of A Ukrainian Sniper
content: NDTV caught up with a sniper from the Second Corps "Khartiia" of the National Guard of Ukraine, Brigade Raid, special operations sniper unit, who spoke about the realities of the job.

title: "Iran To Take The War As Far As Necessary": Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi
content: Iran said it was ready to take the Middle East war "as far as necessary" as it launched strikes across the region Monday, while Trump urged nations to escort tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, warns refusal could hurt NATO's future.

title: Assam To Hold 1st-Ever Single-Phase Poll On April 9: All You Need To Know
content: Assam will witness a single-phase Assembly election for the first time, with polling scheduled for April 9. Chief Election Officer (CEO) Anurag Goel made the announcement on Monday during a press conference in Guwahati.

title: 289 Indian Sailors Repatriated From Gulf Amid Iran War: Government
content: The Directorate General of Shipping has facilitated the safe repatriation of 286 Indian seafarers from the Gulf region so far, including 33 repatriations in the past 48 hours, the government said on Monday.

title: How World's Most Elusive Artist, Banksy, Was Finally Unmasked
content: For decades, Banksy kept the world guessing. The Bristol-born graffiti artist, whose works have sold for millions, built an empire on anonymity. Now, a detailed investigation hints that the mystery might be over.

title: The 4 Mahagathbandhan MLAs Who Skipped Rajya Sabha Voting In Bihar
content: In the Rajya Sabha elections being held for five seats in Bihar, the National Democratic Alliance (NDA) is considered certain to win all five.

title: 2021 Redux? Suvendu Adhikari Takes Fight To Mamata Banerjee's Kolkata Seat
content: The BJP's Suvendu Adhikari, who beat West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee from Nandigram in the last assembly election, wants a rematch -- this time in Bhabanipur, her current constituency.

title: Oldest Known Whale Song Recording May Unlock Mysteries Of The Ocean
content: A haunting whale song discovered on decades-old audio equipment could open up a new understanding of how the huge animals communicate, according to researchers who say it's the oldest such recording known.

title: Air India Flight Makes Precautionary Landing In Ireland Due To 'Technical Snag'
content: The flight, AI102, took off from JFK (New York) Airport and made a precautionary landing in Ireland at 4:30 pm (local time).

title: Devendra Fadnavis Defends Anti-Conversion Bill, Says Law Targets Fraud, Not Faith
content: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis on Monday defended the state government's proposed Maharashtra Freedom of Religion Bill, 2026, asserting that it is designed to curb fraudulent religious conversions.

title: BJP Releases First List Of 47 Names For Kerala Polls
content: The BJP on Monday released its first list of 47 candidates for the April 9 polls, fielding three former Union ministers.

title: LPG Booking App Beats ChatGPT To Top India’s App Store, Play Store Charts
content: The spike in downloads comes as households rush to secure cooking gas after supply concerns linked to the US-Israel war on Iran and tensions around the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most critical oil and gas shipping routes.

title: NDA's Backchannel Offer For Vijay: EPS Deputy Post, 55 Seats, Say Sources
content: The NDA may soon have Vijay's TVK in its ranks, despite the actor's professed ideological antipathy for the BJP. Sources said despite the TVK's categorical denial, discussions have been in progress for the past few days.

title: 5th Iranian Woman Footballer Gives Up Asylum In Australia, Returns Home
content: The Iranian authorities have welcomed the female soccer player's change of heart as a victory against Australia and US President Donald Trump. The Iranians livin in Australia blame pressure from Tehran.
